description: Color palettes extracted from major works across Pollock's career, with emphasis on drip period and classic period paintings.

palettes.name: Drip Period Early
palettes.work_reference: Cathedral (1947), Full Fathom Five (1947), Enchanted Forest (1947)
palettes.emotional_character: Dense, primordial, subconscious emergence. Earthy and cave-like, suggesting deep psychological excavation. The palette evokes pre-verbal sensation and mythic darkness.
palettes.technique_context: Early drip works used house paint poured from cans onto unprimed canvas laid flat on floor. Paint was often enamel-based (Duco), giving luminous quality even in dark hues.

palettes.name: Classic Period Black Silver White
palettes.work_reference: Number 1A 1948, Number 31 1950, Autumn Rhythm 1950, One: Number 31 1950
palettes.emotional_character: Monumental, heroic, kinetic urgency. The high contrast creates maximum visual energy. Suggests the rhythm of jazz, automatic writing, action frozen in time.
palettes.technique_context: Pollock refined his technique using hardened brushes, sticks, and direct pour. Autumn Rhythm is 17 feet wide—scale amplifies the immersive all-over effect.

palettes.name: Blue Poles Palette
palettes.emotional_character: Chaotic exuberance restrained by monumental vertical structures. The poles provide rhythm while the underlying field seethes with contained energy. More festive and complex than pure black-and-white works.
palettes.technique_context: Blue Poles was painted with Lee Krasner's assistance. The poles were added last using boards as guides. Purchased by Australia for $1.3M in 1973.

palettes.name: Lavender Mist Palette
palettes.emotional_character: Ethereal, transcendent, dreamlike. The lavender pervades the entire surface creating an atmospheric shimmer unlike any other Pollock. Suggests mist over water at dawn, or consciousness dissolving.
palettes.technique_context: Greenberg titled it Lavender Mist—Pollock never used that name. The lavender effect comes from the interaction of blue, red, and white paint at the micro scale of individual drip interactions.

palettes.name: Full Fathom Five Palette
palettes.emotional_character: Oceanic, submarine, Shakespearean. Named from The Tempest ('Full fathom five thy father lies'). Contains embedded physical objects: nails, keys, coins, buttons—the painting has literal physical depth.
palettes.technique_context: One of Pollock's first drip paintings. Physical objects embedded in paint surface. Heavy buildup of paint creates actual physical relief. Green dominance is unusual in his oeuvre.
